channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: EACC Survey Shows Kakamega County with Highest Average Bribes in Kenya at Sh79,305
item.description: According to the Kenya National Gender and Corruption Survey 2025 released by the Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC), Kakamega County has the highest average bribe rate in the country at Sh7...

item.title: Court Issues Death Penalties to Four Men Convicted in Ex-MP George Muchai’s Murder Case
item.description: The High Court in Nairobi has handed death sentences to four men found guilty of killing former Kabete Member of Parliament, George Muchai, his two bodyguards, and driver back in 2015. Justice Grace N...

item.title: IRCTC Introduces Bharat Gaurav Pilgrimage Trains from Hyderabad
item.description: The Indian Railway Catering and Tourism Corporation (IRCTC) has announced the launch of special summer Bharat Gaurav Tourist Trains, offering four pilgrimage packages departing from Hyderabad. These t...

item.title: Police Arrest Four Following Discovery of Hundreds of Suspected Stolen Phones
item.description: Police have arrested four individuals in connection with the discovery of hundreds of suspected stolen mobile phones. The exact location of the find hasn’t been officially disclosed, but reports sugge...

item.title: FG Says US Travel Advisory On Nigeria Doesn’t Reflect Real Security Progress
item.description: The Federal Government of Nigeria don come out talk say the recent travel warning wey United States issue no dey show correct picture of how security matter take be for the country. The US government ...

item.title: Kim Chiu and Sofia Andres’ Scene in “The Alibi” Draws Online Buzz
item.description: The latest episode of ABS-CBN's drama series “The Alibi” has caught the attention of online audiences, particularly a scene featuring Kim Chiu and Sofia Andres in a heated confrontation. Fans praised ...

item.title: Netanyahu under fire in Israel over Iran ceasefire agreement amid ongoing Lebanon strikes
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu is facing significant domestic criticism in Israel following a two-week ceasefire deal with Iran, brokered with involvement from Pakistan and the United States. Opposition figures, ...

item.title: Electro Optic Systems fined $4 million for failing to disclose market-sensitive information
item.description: Electro Optic Systems (EOS), a Canberra-based defence and space technology manufacturer, has been fined $4 million by the Federal Court for breaching market disclosure laws. The company, which supplie...

item.title: Mail & Guardian weekly digital newspaper edition for 10 April 2026
item.description: The provided content is the introductory page for the Mail & Guardian's digital edition dated 10 April 2026. It serves as a subscriber access point where readers are greeted with a 'Good morning' mess...

item.title: Disney announces layoff of up to 1,000 employees, primarily from marketing
item.description: Disney has announced plans to lay off up to 1,000 employees, focusing primarily on its marketing department. This restructuring is the first major move under the leadership of the newly appointed CEO,...

item.title: Russia's Submarine Operation Near UK Cables and Pipelines Exposed
item.description: The UK Ministry of Defence has revealed that three Russian submarines conducted a covert operation near undersea cables and pipelines off the UK's north coast. Defence Secretary John Healey confirmed ...

item.title: Max Verstappen’s Engineer Lambiase to Join McLaren After 2028
item.description: Gianpiero Lambiase, Max Verstappen’s long-time race engineer, has confirmed he will leave Red Bull at the end of his contract in 2028. The 45-year-old will take up a new role as McLaren’s chief racing...

item.title: Kenyan MPs Demand Urgent Review of Security After Vihiga Senator Attacked
item.description: A number of Kenyan Members of Parliament have raised serious concerns over the protection of legislators following a violent attack on Vihiga Senator Godfrey Osotsi in Kisumu. The MPs, including Nabii...

item.title: Trump urges Netanyahu to de-escalate as Israel continues attacks in Lebanon amid US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: Following escalating tensions in the Middle East, US President Donald Trump reportedly urged Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to reduce military operations in Lebanon and engage in peace talk...

item.title: Two Japanese men arrested in Batangas over alleged ties to telecom fraud syndicate in Cambodia
item.description: Two Japanese men were arrested in Lian, Batangas on Wednesday evening by Philippine authorities. The Philippine National Police, together with the Bureau of Immigration, carried out the operation arou...

item.title: Tensions rise as Strait of Hormuz closure threatens ceasefire
item.description: The fragile ceasefire in the Middle East faces serious challenges as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed, escalating tensions in the region. US President Donald Trump is urging allies to send warships...

item.title: Delta Police Neutralize Kidnappers, Recover Ransom Money
item.description: Police and military operatives don clear up a kidnapping situation for Delta State. Dem say two suspected kidnappers don meet their waterloo after a serious exchange of fire during a rescue operation....

item.title: Pakistan Plays Key Role in US-Iran De-escalation, Ceasefire Reached
item.description: In a dramatic turn of events, US President Donald Trump has called off planned military action against Iran following intensive discussions with Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and Chief of De...

item.title: India and Mauritius finalize $680 million aid package, focusing on energy security and development
item.description: On April 9, 2026, India and Mauritius concluded agreements to implement projects under a $680 million aid package provided by India. The package aims to enhance economic ties, focusing on infrastructu...

item.title: Former Nova Scotia Chief Crown Attorney Files Lawsuit Over Workplace Treatment and Demotion
item.description: Kathryn Pentz, a former chief Crown attorney in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia, has filed a civil lawsuit against the Nova Scotia government and the province’s Public Prosecution Service. Pentz, who has ove...
